search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG233 mouth breathing adenoid hypertrophy snoring hearing assessment
site:entnet.org adenoid hypertrophy symptoms clinical practice guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: tonsillectomy or sleep-disordered-breathing guidance as a complete adenoid symptom source
automatic adenoid imaging, endoscopy, sleep study, or surgery recommendation
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The workflow is broader than OME and does not establish obstructive sleep-disordered breathing.
No investigation, procedure, treatment, referral, or urgency conclusion is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children under 12 with hearing or nasal symptoms potentially associated with adenoid context; adolescents, adults, isolated nasal obstruction, and sleep-disordered breathing require different evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, paediatric, audiology, or ENT outpatient symptom documentation.
UAE_applicability: NICE paediatric assessment domains require UAE paediatric, audiology, sleep, ENT, and surgical-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG233 exact recognition and reassessment sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG233 remains current and replaced CG60.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact paediatric OME recognition section directly reviews mouth breathing, snoring, nasal obstruction, adenoid history, hearing, communication, balance, functional impact, ENT examination, hearing testing, and tympanometry; it supplies partial evidence only and does not diagnose adenoid hypertrophy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact reassessment section supports clinician-entered review, arranged follow-up, and questions without recommending adenoid investigation or surgery.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, nasal obstruction, mouth breathing, snoring, apnoea, sleep impact, recurrent infection, hearing, speech, allergy, examination, adenoid size, diagnosis, tests, referral, and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
